items.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue documents gaps in the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` documentation introduced by PR #58341 — specifically around eager pre-handler validation of `previous_response_id`/`conversation.id`, new `Code`/`Param` properties on `ResourceNotFoundException`, and the pre-fetch caching behavior when `GetHistoryAsync()` is called.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (1.0.0-beta.3)
**Affected API:** `ResponseContext.GetHistoryAsync()`, `ResourceNotFoundException`, framework-level request validation
**Scenarios:**
- README Troubleshooting section missing pre-handler 404/400 validation error documentation
- Handler implementation guide error table shows `error.code = null` for `ResourceNotFoundException` but `Code`/`Param` are now non-null public properties
- `GetHistoryAsync()` guide section doesn't mention pre-fetch caching (no extra storage round-trip)
- README Key concepts → `ResponseContext` missing the eager validation guarantee

**Root ask:** Update README and `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` to accurately reflect the new framework behavior introduced in PR #58341.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue; no code defects are described. The behavioral changes were introduced in PR #58341 (merged into 1.0.0-beta.3).

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Troubleshooting and Key concepts → ResponseContext sections
2. Review `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Handler Exceptions table and `GetHistoryAsync()` section
3. Cross-check against the PR #58341 changes to ensure the documentation reflects current behavior
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — issue concerns SDK documentation for an Azure client library, not a REST API/service-side defect
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — package `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` lives in `sdk/agentserver/`, which maps to the `%AI Agents` ServiceLabel in CODEOWNERS (line 85)
- **Confidence:** High — explicit package name and service directory in issue body; no ambiguity between service or category
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — the `sdk/agentserver/` path and "AI Agents" service label match unambiguously
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** `@ankitbko` `@RaviPidaparthi`
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; no assignee (ServiceOwners-only path). "needs-team-attention" not added (issue is bot-filed, not customer-reported).
- **Scan notes:** Scanned CODEOWNERS bottom-to-top; no management or other service entries matched "AI Agents". The `%AI Agents` entry at line 85 was the sole match.
</details>
